column1: # Local additions to Skretzo's transport graph.
# Same format as transports.tsv:
#   originX originY originPlane <TAB> destX destY destPlane <TAB> menuOption menuTarget objectID <TAB> Skills <TAB> Items <TAB> Quests <TAB> Varbits <TAB> VarPlayers <TAB> Duration <TAB> Display info
# Add rows here when the collision-debug overlay shows V2's BFS hitting a
# wall that has a real, openable gate/door in-game without a corresponding
# entry in Skretzo's bundled data. Keep rows alphabetically grouped by area.
# Lumbridge chicken pen — south gate (object 1560). Skretzo's upstream
# data doesn't include this gate, so V2's BFS treats the pen as
# fenced-in and can't plan pen→bank routes.
# Lumbridge cow-pen / farm-area south gate (object 1559 — open-state ID;
# closed-state ID may differ, RuneLite's impostor resolver handles state
# changes by object id family). This gate sits along the y=3263/3264
# fence boundary that Skretzo's collision data partitions: town
# (component A) → cow-pen + chicken-pen area (component B). Without
# this bridge, V2 can't plan bank → chicken pen because there's no
# transport crossing the fence. The gate is normally already open
# in-game, so the V2 executor will skip clicking and walk through
# (same "verb absent → treat complete" path used by the chicken pen
# gate above).
# Draynor Manor — p=2 door (object 11470) between the SW spiral-staircase
# landing room and the larger upstairs area where Professor Oddenstein
# paces. Without this entry, V2's BFS partitions the SW room from the
# rest of p=2, so the planner refuses any p=1 → p=2 climb whose final
# leg ends at Oddenstein (TARGET_UNREACHABLE at the post-climb BFS).
# Anchor tile (3108, 3364, p=2) from the click inspector; west-edge
# orientation (passage between x=3107 and x=3108) confirmed by N-edge
# attempt failing with same BFS leg unreachable.
# Draynor Manor — p=1 doors along the path from the south-staircase
# landing to the fish-food room (3109, 3357, p=1). Three door instances
# read from the click inspector during the manor walkthrough; same
# north-edge orientation assumption as the p=2 entry above.
